results.name: Idempotency-Key is optional on /pay
results.detail: /pay works without Idempotency-Key header. Key is opt-in but recommended for client retry safety.

results.name: Same Idempotency-Key replays cached response
results.detail: Second call with same key returns the cached 201 response, no duplicate Transaksi row created.

results.name: Malformed Idempotency-Key rejected
results.detail: Key shorter than 8 chars returns 422. Key with invalid characters returns 422. Regex: /^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{8,128}$/.

results.name: Idempotency-Key persisted as sha256 hash
results.detail: Row in idempotency_keys has key_hash 64 chars (sha256 hex), endpoint 'transactions.pay', status_code 201, response_body stored as JSON.

results.name: Last stock item race serializes
results.detail: Two sequential /pay calls with stock=1, qty=1 each: first 201, second 422 (insufficient stock). lockForUpdate() in decrementStock serializes access.

results.name: Concurrent qty=2 calls with stock=2
results.detail: First 201, second 422. FIFO and lockForUpdate prevent oversell.

results.name: FIFO ordering with reusable sentinel
results.detail: Batches sorted by CASE WHEN kadaluarsa IS NULL THEN '9999-12-31' ELSE kadaluarsa END ASC. qty=7 consumed B-2026 (5) + B-2027 (2), B-REUS untouched.

findings.detail: Idempotency-Key is optional. F-006 design doc claims replay protection, but a client that retries without sending the key will create duplicates. Consider making it required for /pay, or document that the client MUST send it on retry.
Migration has index('created_at') for cleanup cron, but no scheduled job exists in app/Console/. Rows accumulate forever.

TransaksiService::pay has a 3-attempt retry loop for the unique id_transaksi race (F-004 fix), but the retry doesn't actually re-run the create — it just breaks out of the loop. The intended retry is missing. Single unique-key violation during clock-rollover or high concurrency would cause 500.
